holdings.name: Tata Consultancy Services
holdings.description: India's IT bellwether and most valuable IT company. $27B+ annual revenue, 600,000+ employees. Dominant in BFSI, Retail and Life Sciences. GenAI integrated into TCS WisdomNext — sold as AI-augmented IT outsourcing to Fortune 1000 clients.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest IT firm. Infosys Topaz AI platform enables clients to build enterprise AI applications. Large deal wins in cloud migration and core banking transformation. 30-year record of dividend payments, regular buybacks.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: 3rd largest with unique Products & Platforms (HCL Software) segment. ERPNext, DRYiCE AIOps, Calpont analytics — recurring SaaS revenue. Engineering R&D services (Volvo, BMW, Airbus) growing with EV and autonomous vehicle tech spending.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: India's 4th largest IT. Under Thierry Delaporte's restructuring — customer-centric model, fewer verticals. Capco acquisition (fintech consulting) and Edgile (cybersecurity) add high-margin consulting revenue. EBITDA margins recovering.

holdings.name: Tech Mahindra
holdings.description: 5G telecom IT specialist — network virtualisation (5G CNF/VNF), OSS/BSS modernisation for AT&T, Comcast, Telstra. Also strong in engineering (aerospace, auto). 'Rise with Tech' transformation under Mohit Joshi rebuilding revenue growth.

holdings.name: LTIMindtree
holdings.description: L&T's marquee IT company formed by LTI + Mindtree merger. Strong in BFSI (especially capital markets), travel & hospitality (Amadeus, Sabre integration) and manufacturing. Canvas platform accelerates cloud and data modernisation for large enterprises.
